title: Crypto News — July 23, 2026: today's crypto market

html:
CRYPTO NEWS — July 23, 2026

Bitcoin coughed up 0.80% overnight while the broader market dipped 0.30%, signaling cautious sellers in a low-conviction environment. The Fear & Greed index slipped from 33 to 31—deeper into fear territory—but positioning remains balanced: perpetual funding rates are flat across BTC, ETH, and SOL with no extreme leverage buildup. This is a standoff. Bitcoin trades dead-center in its 24h range; alts are consolidating. The real test comes at the barriers: BTC above 66,340 signals reversal, below 65,327 invites further pain.

TODAY'S HIGHLIGHTS

Bridge attacks drain $35M in six hours
Three crypto bridges and cross-chain protocols were hit, with Verus, B² Network and others compromised through keys, upgrade powers and validation checks. An attacker used the bridge import path to trigger unbacked Ethereum-side payouts, draining $7.54M in ETH, tBTC, USDC, USDT, EURC, MKR, and scrvUSD. This is bridge risk on full display—same vulnerability exploited twice. Your move: cross-chain exposure becomes liability in bear markets. If you're holding tokens wrapped on secondary chains, verify audits and consider consolidating to main-layer assets. DeFi bridges remain infrastructure weak links.

ETF inflows stabilize after May bleed-out
Bitcoin ETFs logged positive net inflows last week for the second consecutive week, the first time since May. This is the first crack in the door. The inflows did not cover the outflows over the past several weeks, so we're still in hole-filling mode, but the trend matters. Watch whether this momentum holds or rolls over on the next macro headline. Small inflows don't make a bull market, but they do prevent free-fall.

SOL ETF flows show selective institutional interest
Bitwise's Solana ETF saw $2.64M in net inflows, bringing cumulative inflows to $1.1399 billion. Selective buying suggests institutions are making tactical allocations rather than broad market moves. Altcoin flows remain cherry-picked; there's no indiscriminate alt season yet. SOL's strength on technical charts (bearish price, bullish on-chain) creates a mismatch—traders should wait for price confirmation before betting the narrative flip is real.

WATCH LIST

Bitcoin's 24h high (66,340) is the bull catalyst; the 24h low (65,327) is the bear trap. Hold either and you've got direction. Ethereum and Solana underperforming BTC's relative strength means dominance and rotation risk remain live—watch if alts can reclaim yesterday's levels or if they roll into fresh lows. Fear gauge at 31 leaves room to drop; capitulation typically prints in the 10-20 range, so we're not there yet. Oil topped $85 per barrel overnight (inflation backdrop), and geopolitical noise around U.S.–Iran tensions could spike risk-off sentiment fast. Stablecoin supply steady at $257.2B; no signal of aggressive accumulation below. The bridge attack news will likely resurface on-chain risk conversations—any new exploit headlines could trigger sharp deleveraging in derivative markets.

See you tomorrow — stay sharp, watch the barriers, and size down on cross-chain plays.
